q: The two steps in protein synthesis are ___ and translation.
In transcription, mRNA is synthesized based on the ___ template of a gene.
The central ___ of molecular biology stated that genetic information flows from DNA to RNA to Protein.
Transcription occurs in the ___ of the cell.
The three defined stages in the transcription process are initiation, ___, and termination.
For each gene, only one strand of the double-stranded DNA molecule is transcribed, called the ___.
The strand which is not transcribed is called the ___ or coding strand.
During elongation, the ___ complex works its way along the DNA molecule.
The newly synthesized mRNA is called ___ or pre-mRNA.
Modification of mRNA includes the addition of a $5'$ cap and a ___ tail at $3'$ end.
The removal of ___ (non-coding regions) with specific enzyme is a necessary modification.
The remaining ___ (coding regions) are joined after intron removal.
The process of removal of introns and joining of exons is called ___.
The second stage of protein synthesis involves ___ which occurs in the cytoplasm.
Translation is the conversion of a sequence of nucleotides into a sequence of amino acids in the ___.
There are three binding sites for tRNA on the large subunit of ribosome: ___, P site and E site.
Each tRNA carries the specific amino acid at its ___.
Opposite to the acceptor stem is the ___ which connects to the specific codon on the mRNA.
The small ribosomal sub-unit attaches to the mRNA near the ___ (AUG).
The first tRNA that binds to the codon is the initiator tRNA with its ___ anticodon.
During elongation, a tRNA with an attached polypeptide is in the ___, then peptide bond is formed.
A tRNA carrying the next amino acid enters the ___.
The mRNA moves forward by ___, and the polypeptide-bearing tRNA is now at the ribosome P site.
The ___ tRNA exits translation from the E site.
The termination phase begins when a ___ codon on the mRNA is reached.
A protein, called a ___, cleaves (cuts) the polypeptide from the last tRNA.
